edges.description: Genetic and unique environmental liability can converge on stress physiology and autonomic regulation.
edges.source: Polygenic Liability and Individually Unique Environmental Risk

edges.description: Familial and unique-environmental liability are modeled upstream of altered autonomic regulation, including heart-rate variability changes.
edges.source: Polygenic Liability and Individually Unique Environmental Risk

edges.description: Familial and environmental liability is modeled upstream of emotion-regulation circuit dysfunction.
edges.source: Polygenic Liability and Individually Unique Environmental Risk

edges.description: Altered emotion-regulation circuitry is modeled as a proximal contributor to affective instability.
Impaired regulation and salience control are represented as contributors to impulsive behavioral dysregulation.
Disturbed self/identity and interpersonal functioning are represented as downstream clinical manifestations.
Reduced or altered autonomic flexibility is modeled as a proximal contributor to affective instability.
Autonomic dysregulation is modeled as contributing to impulsive behavioral dysregulation.
Autonomic dysregulation is modeled as part of the pathway toward cardiovascular and metabolic vulnerability.
Autonomic and inflammatory dysregulation are modeled as contributors to increased cardiovascular and metabolic vulnerability in BPD.

nodes.description: Altered heart-rate variability is represented as an autonomic regulatory mechanism associated with BPD emotional instability and impulsivity.

evidence.reference_title: Metabolic Dysfunctions, Dysregulation of the Autonomic Nervous System, and Echocardiographic Parameters in Borderline Personality Disorder: A Narrative Review
evidence.snippet: Altered heart rate variability (HRV), reflecting the dysregulation of the autonomic nervous system, is associated with some BPD core symptoms, such as emotional instability and impulsivity.

nodes.description: BPD is associated with metabolic and cardiovascular risk markers including BMI, blood pressure, inflammatory markers, and altered autonomic function.

evidence.reference_title: Metabolic Dysfunctions, Dysregulation of the Autonomic Nervous System, and Echocardiographic Parameters in Borderline Personality Disorder: A Narrative Review
evidence.snippet: Metabolic dysfunctions in BPD, such as elevated body mass index (BMI), high blood pressure, and inflammatory markers like C-reactive protein (CRP), exacerbate these risks.

nodes.description: Dialectical behavior therapy is a structured psychotherapy with randomized trial evidence for suicidality, self-injury, mood instability, and related BPD outcomes.

evidence.reference_title: Efficacy of Dialectical Behavior Therapy in the Treatment of Borderline Personality Disorder: A Systematic Review of Randomized Controlled Trials.
evidence.snippet: We found 18 RCTs, most of which supported the effectiveness of DBT for BPD.

evidence.reference_title: Efficacy of Dialectical Behavior Therapy in the Treatment of Borderline Personality Disorder: A Systematic Review of Randomized Controlled Trials.
evidence.snippet: Most studies revealed that both short-term DBT and standard DBT improved suicidality in BPD patients with small or moderate effect sizes, lasting up to 24 months after the treatment period.

nodes.description: BPD involves unstable self/identity, emotional dysregulation, impulsivity, and disturbed interpersonal relationships, represented as a clinical dysregulation node downstream of inherited and environmental liability.

evidence.reference_title: Metabolic Dysfunctions, Dysregulation of the Autonomic Nervous System, and Echocardiographic Parameters in Borderline Personality Disorder: A Narrative Review
evidence.snippet: Borderline personality disorder (BPD) is a complex psychiatric disorder characterized by an unstable sense of self and identity, emotional dysregulation, impulsivity, and disturbed interpersonal relationships.

nodes.description: Rapid shifts in affect and intense emotional instability.

evidence.reference_title: Metabolic Dysfunctions, Dysregulation of the Autonomic Nervous System, and Echocardiographic Parameters in Borderline Personality Disorder: A Narrative Review
evidence.snippet: Altered heart rate variability (HRV), reflecting the dysregulation of the autonomic nervous system, is associated with some BPD core symptoms, such as emotional instability and impulsivity.

nodes.description: Manualized psychotherapies are first-line treatments for BPD, with several modalities showing benefit relative to treatment as usual.

evidence.reference_title: Psychotherapies for the treatment of borderline personality disorder: A systematic review.
evidence.snippet: Practice guidelines recommend psychotherapies as first-line treatments.

evidence.reference_title: Psychotherapies for the treatment of borderline personality disorder: A systematic review.
evidence.snippet: All commonly used psychotherapies improve BPD severity, symptoms, and functioning.

nodes.description: BPD includes disturbed self/identity and interpersonal functioning.

evidence.reference_title: Metabolic Dysfunctions, Dysregulation of the Autonomic Nervous System, and Echocardiographic Parameters in Borderline Personality Disorder: A Narrative Review
evidence.snippet: Borderline personality disorder (BPD) is a complex psychiatric disorder characterized by an unstable sense of self and identity, emotional dysregulation, impulsivity, and disturbed interpersonal relationships.

nodes.description: Impulsive behavior is a core clinical feature.

evidence.reference_title: Metabolic Dysfunctions, Dysregulation of the Autonomic Nervous System, and Echocardiographic Parameters in Borderline Personality Disorder: A Narrative Review
evidence.snippet: Altered heart rate variability (HRV), reflecting the dysregulation of the autonomic nervous system, is associated with some BPD core symptoms, such as emotional instability and impulsivity.

nodes.description: Mentalization-based treatment is represented as a psychotherapy option that can improve psychiatric symptoms, functioning, and mentalizing capacity.

evidence.reference_title: Mentalization based treatment for a broad range of personality disorders: a naturalistic study
evidence.snippet: Several studies have observed that mentalization-based treatment (MBT) is an effective treatment for borderline personality disorder (BPD), but its effectiveness for other personality disorders (PDs) has hardly been examined.

evidence.reference_title: Mentalization based treatment for a broad range of personality disorders: a naturalistic study
evidence.snippet: These results suggest that MBT coincides with symptomatic and functional improvement across a broad range of PDs and shows that MBT is associated with improvements in mentalizing capacity.

nodes.description: BPD is represented as a multifactorial disorder with familial aggregation, substantial heritability, and major contribution from non-shared environmental exposures.
nodes.id: Polygenic Liability and Individually Unique Environmental Risk

evidence.reference_title: Familial risk and heritability of diagnosed borderline personality disorder: a register study of the Swedish population
evidence.snippet: Heritability was estimated at 46% (95% CI = 39–53), and the remaining variance was explained by individually unique environmental factors.

nodes.description: Self-harm risk is markedly elevated in diagnosed BPD.

evidence.reference_title: Borderline personality disorder: associations with psychiatric disorders, somatic illnesses, trauma, and adverse behaviors
evidence.snippet: Other notable findings from Cox regressions include psychotic disorders (HR 95% CI 24.48 [23.14–25.90]), epilepsy (3.38 [3.08–3.70]), violent crime victimization (7.65 [7.25–8.06]), and self-harm (17.72 [17.27–18.19]).

nodes.description: Stress physiology in BPD includes altered autonomic regulation, HPA-axis dysregulation, chronic inflammation, allostatic load, and cardiometabolic risk.

evidence.reference_title: Metabolic Dysfunctions, Dysregulation of the Autonomic Nervous System, and Echocardiographic Parameters in Borderline Personality Disorder: A Narrative Review
evidence.snippet: Dysregulation of the hypothalamic–pituitary–adrenal (HPA) axis, often stemming from early trauma, contributes to chronic inflammation and elevated allostatic load, which further increases cardiovascular risk.
